Citizen Participation No members of the public signed up to speak during the public comment period. Petitioner Presentation — Wisdom Idollet Resident, Montgomery Trace Section 4 Mr. Idollet presented on behalf of the neighborhood. Key points: - Neighborhood profile: Approximately 80 homes; borders Montgomery ISD to the north and west; accessible only via Hempie Egypt Road, which is otherwise entirely within Montgomery ISD. - Geographic anomaly: The CISD boundary appears to have crossed a creek specifically to include this neighborhood, which Mr. Idollet described as historically unusual. - Bus routing: CISD buses must travel 4–8 extra miles through other districts to serve the neighborhood. - Petition signatures: 53% of registered voters signed meeting the TEC majority threshold ; approximately 63% of actual current residents signed. About 12% were never contacted. - Reasons cited by residents for wanting to transfer: - Montgomery ISD schools are closer on average - Most residents already socialize, shop, and attend church in Montgomery - Perceived safety advantage near Montgomery schools based on CrimeGrades.org - Higher proficiency/SAT scores at Montgomery schools they would be zoned to - Bus routing inefficiency for CISD Challenges acknowledged by Mr. Idollet: 1. Property value ratio: The neighborhood falls approximately 4 students short of meeting the TEC ratio requirement. He noted that a majority of CISD board members signing the petition could satisfy this requirement under TEC. 2. Meets and bounds description: CISD legal counsel found the petition's legal description inadequate. Mr. Idollet acknowledged the petitioners are not lawyers or surveyors and worked from the neighborhood plat. 3. Debt transfer: Montgomery ISD expressed concern about inheriting CISD debt associated with the territory. Board Questions for Mr. Idollet - Trustee unidentified : Asked whether residents were aware of the tax rate increase. Mr. Idollet confirmed all signatories were informed; average annual increase would be approximately $568 per household . Most were agreeable; only 2–3 families objected on those grounds. Residents over 65 with locked-in taxes were generally supportive. - Trustee: Asked about Montgomery ISD's open enrollment policy. Mr. Idollet clarified that Montgomery ISD closed its full open enrollment 1–2 years ago and now has a limited, application-based process. He stated the decision before the board was partly premised on open enrollment still being available, which he said is no longer the case. - Trustee: Asked how many K–12 children are in the neighborhood. Mr. Idollet estimated 22 currently enrolled in CISD , with some already transferred to Montgomery ISD before open enrollment closed, and some homeschooling families. - Trustee: Asked about the historical reason the boundary crossed the creek. No one present had that information. District Presentation — General Counsel Key points presented on behalf of CISD: - Montgomery ISD voted against the annexation , citing concerns about inheriting CISD debt and other issues. - Tax rate impact: CISD's tax rate is currently lower than Montgomery ISD's. The average home in the area is valued at approximately $420,000, resulting in an estimated $568/year increase per homeowner if annexed. - Assessed value of territory: Approximately $50 million assessed; approximately $38 million taxable after exemptions . - 94 total parcels in the subdivision per Montgomery Central Appraisal District records. - 26 students were identified as currently riding CISD buses from the subdivision this school year; however, the statute requires using the prior year's last day of school enrollment figure — 22 students — for the ratio calculation. - Statutory ratio requirement TEC Chapter 13 : The ratio of taxable property value of the territory to total CISD taxable value must not exceed the ratio of school-age children in the territory to total CISD enrollment. In this case, the property value ratio is higher than the student ratio , meaning the statutory threshold is not met and the board lacks authority to proceed. - Montgomery ISD transfer option: General Counsel noted Montgomery ISD appears to still have a transfer application online. Mr. Idollet disputed this, stating full open enrollment has ended and the current process is limited and application-based. - Debt transfer question: A trustee asked whether CISD could simply retain the debt rather than transfer it. General Counsel was researching the statute in real time and could not give a firm answer. Staff noted that if CISD retains the debt but loses the taxable value and ADA, it would face a "double whammy" — losing both revenue streams while retaining the obligation. Board Discussion - Trustees discussed the historical oddity of the boundary crossing the creek and noted CISD has similar situations elsewhere e.g., buses traveling through Splendora ISD to reach Candy Creek area . - Trustees noted concern that the open enrollment premise cited in the district's presentation may be inaccurate based on Mr. Idollet's information. - One trustee highlighted the need for a firm legal answer on whether the debt must transfer before any future action could be considered. Motion and Vote Motion made by Board President : The board determine that the petition filed by Wisdom Idollet seeking detachment of Montgomery Trace Section 4 from Conroe ISD and annexation to Montgomery ISD fails to satisfy the mandatory property value ratio requirement set forth in Texas Education Code Chapter 13, including §§13.03 and 13.051, in that the ratio of taxable property value of the territory proposed to be detached to the taxable property value of the affected district does not meet the statutory threshold; and further that the board adopt the findings of fact and resolution reflecting that determination and decline to proceed with the requested detachment and annexation. Vote: 6–0 in favor motion carries Outcome: The petition was denied on statutory grounds. The hearing was formally concluded. Discussion: - Trustee May expressed enthusiasm for the virtual school, noting the passage of enabling legislation and education savings accounts ESAs , calling it a "boutique style learning" opportunity. - A board member suggested incorporating "The" in the name — "The Conroe Virtual School" TCVS — to align with other district schools e.g., The Woodlands High School, College Park and to avoid the abbreviation "CVS" being confused with the pharmacy chain. - Multiple members expressed support for TCVS.
